display_text: Mage Slayer, Cost: 2800: Places a debuff on attacked enemies, dealing 20 magical damage per second and reducing their spell damage by 40% for 3 seconds. This provides sustained magic damage and an anti-caster effect, making it a powerful counter-pick tool in fights. Stats: CD: N/A (passive), Spell Damage Debuff: 40%, Debuff Duration: 3s, DPS: 20.

display_text: Mage Slayer, Cost: 2800: Provides balanced stats focused on survivability and mana sustain against magical damage: +20% magic resistance, +8 damage, +30 attack speed, +5 health regen, and +2 mana regen. This profile makes the wielder tankier against spells while enhancing their right-click potential. Stats: MR: +20%, Damage: +8, AS: +30, HP Regen: +5, Mana Regen: +2.

display_text: Mage Slayer, Cost: 2800: Acts as a teamfight debuff aura, applying a significant spell damage reduction on primary targets to protect your team. By reducing enemy spell output by 40%, it directly counters magic-heavy lineups and AoE casters. The damage-over-time effect provides additional zoning pressure. Stats: Spell Debuff: 40%, Team Utility: High.
search_text: mage slayer teamfight, anti-mage teamfight, team debuff, aura debuff, protect team from magic, magic damage reduction teamfight, counter AoE spells, team magic defense, Mage Slayer

display_text: Mage Slayer, Cost: 2800: A core situational pickup against teams with high magic damage output, such as spell-based nukers, intelligence cores, and AoE ability users. The 40% spell damage reduction debuff severely cripples their primary damage source. Prioritize this when enemy teamfight revolves around magical burst. Stats: Counter: Magic Nukers, Intelligence Cores.
search_text: mage slayer situational, vs magic damage, vs casters, vs intelligence heroes, vs nukers, counter magic lineup, anti-spell item, situational magic defense, Mage Slayer

display_text: Mage Slayer, Cost: 2800: Synergizes well with high-attack-speed heroes who can apply the debuff consistently to multiple targets, and with heroes who already build into its components (Orb of Venom, Cornucopia). It's a natural fit for agility carries or tempo cores facing magical threats. The buildup is smooth through early-game components.
